qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: vp migrate splices raw JSON config bytes into executable vite.config code
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: This finding is raw-JSON-to-JavaScript injection in `repo/crates/vite_migration/src/vite_config.rs` and `repo/packages/cli/src/migration/migrator.ts`, whereas the advisory is version-string path traversal in `downloadPackageManager()` and `vite_install::download_package_manager`.

qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Deeply nested run config causes a stack-overflow abort in static config extraction
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: This finding is recursive AST-descent stack overflow in `repo/crates/vite_static_config/src/lib.rs`, not attacker-controlled directory traversal during package-manager download and install, so the vulnerable code and control flow do not match the advisory.

target_advisory_judgement.reason: No qualifying finding identifies the advisory's vulnerable `vite-plus/binding` `downloadPackageManager()` path or its root cause of using an untrusted package-manager version string as a filesystem path component under `VP_HOME`.
evidence: The advisory describes `vite-plus/binding` `downloadPackageManager()` accepting an untrusted `version` string and using it in filesystem paths under `VP_HOME/package_manager/<pm>/<version>/...`, enabling traversal outside `VP_HOME`.

A disposable source inspection under `.judge` confirmed that `packages/cli/binding/src/package_manager.rs` forwards `options.version` into `vite_install::download_package_manager`, and `crates/vite_install/src/package_manager.rs` constructs `target_dir = home_dir.join("package_manager").join(&bin_name).join(&version)` for installs.

Neither of the two qualifying findings reference `packages/cli/binding`, `downloadPackageManager()`, `VP_HOME`, `vite_install::download_package_manager`, or package-manager install path construction; they instead cover migration config code injection and static-config stack-overflow DoS.

The only session artifact in the archive/path family is a `no_vuln` review of `vite_js_runtime` and `vite_setup` extraction flows, which is not a qualifying finding and does not cover the advisory's binding/package-manager root cause.
